# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Create a Docker Compose configuration for RabbitMQ deployment. Define RabbitMQ service with management plugin enabled. Expose management UI port for monitoring and administration. Configure environment variables for cluster setup. This is for our production message broker infrastructure.
# Category: rabbitmq_management_exposed

version: '3'
services:
  rabbitmq101:
    image: rabbitmq:3-management # The official RabbitMQ docker image
    restart: always # Automatically start RabbitMQ when the container is restarted (this is necessary for clustering and in production mode. This does not mean that containers automatically stop or re-start in production. To do so you must make sure that they are properly shut down).
    hostname: rabbitmq101 # Define a host name which is used to create the network address. It can be accessed through the docker container using 172.19.43.58
    environment:
      - RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER=admin
      - RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASS=<INSERT PASSWORD HERE> # This is the admin password for RabbitMQ management UI
      - RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_VHOST=/
    ports:
      - 172.19.43.58:5672-5675:5672-5675/tcp
|>
